snippet.title: 2022 CED Distinguished Leadership Awards: Albert Bourla
snippet.description:
Albert Bourla, the Chairman and CEO of Pfizer Inc., accepts the CED Distinguished Leadership Award.

About the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards:
https://www.conference-board.org/events/CED-Distinguished-Leadership-Awards-Dinner

For more than a quarter century, the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards have been honoring business leaders and their companies who exemplify CED’s mission by answering the call for corporate citizenship and demonstrating a strong commitment to public policy in the nation’s interests.

The CED Annual Distinguished Leadership Awards Celebration pays special tribute to business leaders and their companies who have demonstrated exceptional leadership in very challenging times and have helped our nation and the communities where they operate navigate the many challenges before us, including: defeating the pandemic, ensuring an economic recovery that provides equal opportunity for all Americans building a more civil and just society and upholding a rules-based international order.

The Awards Celebration commemorates the legacy of CED’s founders, America’s leading CEOs who, during WWII, heeded the call to public service to guide the transition from a wartime economy to a prosperous peacetime economy. Today, CED is fulfilling its mission by working to help ensure the nation recovers from the current crises with an economy that provides equal opportunity for all Americans.

snippet.title: Klaus Schwab and Albert Bourla Admitted this at DAVOS 2022
snippet.description:
Klaus Schwab DAVOS 2022

🔔 Subscribe now with all notifications on for more Klaus Schwab, Davos Agenda and The Great Reset

 How many of you are familiar with the names #klausschwab  and #AlbertBourla ? For those who don't know them, let me introduce them. Klaus Schwab is the #worldeconomicforum  's founder
and the #WEF 's current CEO. During the #pandemic times, which #bigpharma company produced thelargest amount of vaccines? Yes, no one can forget that. It's #Pfizer. Albert Bourla is the sitting chairman and CEO of this American #pharmaceutical  company. He was made the CEO in 2019, just before the #covid crisis broke out. Because of the same reason, he got a lot of credit for facing the #pandemic situation brilliantly. 

Now, are you wondering why two people from completely different arenas are introduced together? Before that, let me ask you, are you aware
of what happens during the WEF meetings in the Swiss city of #davos ?
As responsible social animals, we have the right to know about global economic trends; this is what the annual WEF meetings are meant for. Do you think the Davos meeting investigates the
#truth  and brings it to the public? WEF also held #Davos2022, and we will discuss the same today. But more precisely, we are concentrating on the things Pfizer CEO and the WEF CEO himself admitted during the 2022 convention.

📺 Watch the entire video for more information!

snippet.title: Pfizer Presents: Inclusive Research, More Equitable Medicine
snippet.description:
The COVID-19 pandemic forced the world to grapple with a long-standing truth: that poor diversity in early and late stage medical research remains a major threat to health equity. Overcoming barriers and challenges to fair representation in research and development will not happen overnight, nor can it be achieved by a single institution. In order to pioneer lasting and sustainable changes in the way we conduct R&D, pharmaceutical companies and the broader research, medical, and patient communities need to come together—not only to assess current practices, but also to identify and address drivers of disparity. How can we apply the recent lessons of scientific innovations discovered in the pandemic to advance health equity?

This conversation was part of Aspen Ideas: Health 2022.

Speakers: 
Donna Cryer, Founder, President, and CEO, Global Liver Institute

Leana Wen, Public Health Professor, George Washington University; Contributing Columnist, Washington Post; CNN Medical Analyst

Aida Habtezion, Chief Medical Officer and Head of Worldwide Medical and Safety, Pfizer

Gary Puckrein, President and CEO, National Minority Quality Forum


Aspen Ideas: Health takes place on the Aspen Institute’s wondrous 40-acre campus in the Rocky Mountains from June 22-25, 2022. The three-day event, which has opened the renowned Aspen Ideas Festival since 2014, is where health’s biggest challenges meet its biggest thinkers. #AspenIdeasHealth #AspenIdeas

For more information about Aspen Ideas: Health, visit https://www.aspenideas.org/attend/health

snippet.title: What Does it Mean to "TRUST The Science"?
snippet.description: After Briahna interviewed Dr. Vinay Prasad last month, some listeners, along with tweeters from further afield, recommended other COVID experts to offer a different view. This week, Briahna interviewed one of those people -- Dr. Justin Feldman, a social epidemiologist from Harvard's School of Public Health. She's also joined by Jamie Seltzer, Director of Scientific and Medical Research at #MeAction. I asked them about Prasad's central claim that advice around boosting requires more nuance as we learn more about the limitations of vaccines, how long covid factors into policy planning, and what it even means these days to "trust science" now that the CDC has gotten so much wrong.

snippet.title: 2022 CED Distinguished Leadership Awards: James Quincey
snippet.description:
James Quincey, the Chairman and CEO of The Coca-Cola Company, introduces award honoree Albert Bourla, the Chairman and CEO of Pfizer Inc.

About the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards:
https://www.conference-board.org/events/CED-Distinguished-Leadership-Awards-Dinner

For more than a quarter century, the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards have been honoring business leaders and their companies who exemplify CED’s mission by answering the call for corporate citizenship and demonstrating a strong commitment to public policy in the nation’s interests.

The CED Annual Distinguished Leadership Awards Celebration pays special tribute to business leaders and their companies who have demonstrated exceptional leadership in very challenging times and have helped our nation and the communities where they operate navigate the many challenges before us, including: defeating the pandemic, ensuring an economic recovery that provides equal opportunity for all Americans building a more civil and just society and upholding a rules-based international order.

The Awards Celebration commemorates the legacy of CED’s founders, America’s leading CEOs who, during WWII, heeded the call to public service to guide the transition from a wartime economy to a prosperous peacetime economy. Today, CED is fulfilling its mission by working to help ensure the nation recovers from the current crises with an economy that provides equal opportunity for all Americans.

snippet.title: Financing the New Economy
snippet.description:
Disruption won't be enough. The shift to a more digitally driven, resilient and sustainable economy -- especially one operating at scale -- encompasses a transition from old to new. And because so much of that transition is capital-intensive, in everything from heavy industry to shipping, it'll require huge commitments of equity and debt financing over the coming decades. Who's best positioned to make the necessary commitments? What if any trade-offs does such financing entail? Bloomberg's Sarah Wells moderates a panel with Hazem Ben-Gacem, Co-Chief Executive Officer, Investcorp, Anne Richards, Chief Executive Officer, Fidelity International, Bill Winters, Group Chief Executive, Standard Chartered and Lei Zhang, Founder, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Hillhouse Investments at the 2022 Bloomberg New Economy Forum in Singapore.

snippet.title: ⭐ Strategic Outlook: Responsible Consumption [Visión Estratégica: Consumo Responsable] #WEF22 #Davos
snippet.description:
⭐ DESCRIPTION: By 2030, the global consumer class will exceed 5 billion people and spending is projected to reach nearly $100 trillion, representing 50% growth since 2020. What new business models, policies and incentives are needed to responsibly and inclusively advance consumption for a healthier planet and people?

⭐ DESCRIPCIÓN: Para 2030, la clase de consumidores globales superará los 5 mil millones de personas y se proyecta que el gasto alcance casi $ 100 billones, lo que representa un crecimiento del 50 % desde 2020. ¿Qué nuevos modelos de negocios, políticas e incentivos se necesitan para avanzar de manera responsable e inclusiva en el consumo para un planeta más saludable y ¿gente?

⭐ SPEAKERS:
📌 Jane Nelson. Director, Corporate Responsibility Initiative, Harvard Kennedy School of Government.
📌 Vivianne Heijnen. Minister for the Environment, Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management of the Netherlands.
📌 Nicolas Hieronimus. Chief Executive Officer, L'Oréal.
📌 J. Michael Evans. President, Alibaba Group Holding Limited.
📌 Gilberto Tomazoni. Global Chief Executive Officer, JBS S/A.

⭐ CATEGORY:
📌 Better Business / Mejores negocios #WEF22 #Davos 
📌 World Economic Forum

snippet.title: Microchips In Pills To Force Compliance Are REAL Says Pfizer CEO
snippet.description:
Pfizer CEO and former veterinarian Philip Bourla recently appeared on a panel where he touted the advent of pills containing microchips that would alert healthcare professionals, hospitals and insurance companies when patients had consumed their prescribed medications. “Imagine the compliance,” the Big Pharma oligarch effused, seemingly unaware that he was validating the fears of so-called “conspiracy theorists” who have been warning about just this eventuality.

Jimmy and American comedian Kurt Metzger discuss the Pfizer CEO’s delight at the prospect of Pharma gaining monitoring capability over its customers’ medication usage.

snippet.title: Leadership in Challenging Times: A Conversation with Penny Pennington, Edward Jones
snippet.description:
https://www.conference-board.org/events/CED-Distinguished-Leadership-Awards-Dinner

“We look back to history to know that the times of tumult will pass, but we also have to move through them in really productive ways…to put things in historical perspective, but also to put things into perspective by helping people take a long-term view.”  That’s according to Penny Pennington, Managing Partner of Edward Jones, in an interview with Dr. Lori Esposito Murray of the Committee for Economic Development, the public policy center of The Conference Board (CED).

This CED discussion series, Leadership in Challenging Times, interviews business leaders about the unprecedented challenges facing the nation and how they are helping chart a path forward for both their companies and communities in which they operate.  In this thought-provoking discussion, Penny Pennington discusses with Dr. Murray the leadership qualities that are needed in these challenging times, what history tells us and what the CEO’s role requires in the public sphere.

“We look back to history to know that the times of tumult will pass, but we also have to move through them in really productive ways…to put things in historical perspective, but also to put things into perspective by helping people take a long-term view.”  That’s according to Penny Pennington, Managing Partner of Edward Jones, in an interview with Dr. Lori Esposito Murray of the Committee for Economic Development, the public policy center of The Conference Board (CED).

This CED discussion series, Leadership in Challenging Times, interviews business leaders about the unprecedented challenges facing the nation and how they are helping chart a path forward for both their companies and communities in which they operate.  In this thought-provoking discussion, Penny Pennington discusses with Dr. Murray the leadership qualities that are needed in these challenging times, what history tells us and what the CEO’s role requires in the public sphere.

snippet.description:
Organizations are learning that content moderation processes by humans alone cannot scale to meet safety, regulatory, and operational needs in the user-generated content era. AI can help organizations across industries reclaim up to 95 percent of time spent moderating content manually and streamline the detection and protection of PII and PHI across environments and processes. Join this chalk talk to explore how to scale to efficiently moderate high volumes of user-generated content, keep online communities safe and inclusive, and save content moderation time and costs. snippet.title: The Future of Medicine Podcast
snippet.description:
David Rhew (Chief Medical Officer at Microsoft), Christina Farr (Health Tech Lead at OMERS Ventures), and Steven Chan (Stanford School of Medicine) are interviewed by instructor Ernestine Fu. 

In the last few decades, we have seen incredible innovations in medicine. We have been able to eradicate smallpox, learned how to transplant organs, and even created artificial hearts. In less than a year, we were able to develop multiple vaccines to COVID-19 (vaccines typically take at least a decade to develop) that have an unprecedented level of overall immunity. While recent advancements have been impressive, we still have a long way to go. The future of medicine will have even more profound impacts on our daily lives. In this session, we explore what recent advances have been made and what the future holds for us. 

Topics covered include COVID-19 and vaccine passports, artificial intelligence and unstructured data sources, telehealth and voice user interfaces, investment opportunities, and the broader issues of health disparity.

snippet.title: Experts Explain: What is AI? | Computer scientist Stuart Russell | WEF
snippet.description:
Stuart Russell is a world-leading computer scientist and a professor at the University of California, Berkeley. He’s also the author of the book Human Compatible. In this in-depth video interview, Professor Russell addresses our collective unease around the rise of artificial intelligence, exploring the impact on jobs and the scope of ‘general purpose’ AI, from the humble domestic thermostat to the attention-hungry bleeps and pings that keep us glued to our smartphones.

“If you nudge somebody hundreds of times a day for days on end, you can move them a long way in terms of their beliefs, their preferences, their opinions,” says Russell. “Algorithms are having a massive effect on billions of people in the world. I think we’ve given them a free pass for far too long.

You can also listen to Stuart Russell debating the promise and peril of AI in this podcast interview: https://open.spotify.com/episode/0VLO9mldvzsWu9MYEYbujc?si=hFJwG6mdTwarfyGwPLGhuw

The World Economic Forum is the International Organization for Public-Private Cooperation. The Forum engages the foremost political, business, cultural and other leaders of society to shape global, regional and industry agendas. snippet.title: Pfizer’s Biotech Strategy: When a “Market Force” Partners with a “Market Mover”
snippet.description:
The synergistic relationship between biotech's and biopharma's can dramatically change the way new drugs and vaccines are bought to market – helping advance innovation on BOTH sides in a very mutually beneficial way. I’ve got an inside look at how Pfizer is working with emerging biotech start-ups, thanks to this in-depth chat with Pfizer’s Senior Vice President of Business Innovation, Kathy Fernando.

Kathy is not only responsible for developing relationships with biotech's on behalf of Pfizer, BUT during the pandemic she led Pfizer’s mRNA scientific strategy, which was integral to its ability to rapidly develop the Covid-19 vaccine. We geek out on the “cool science” that mRNA is – AND the new platforms that biotech's are bringing to the table – and talk about the impact both are making on the business of Big Pharma, the hot biotech investment space, and, most importantly, patients.

We also get into a bigger conversation about innovation in the Life Sciences industry – with great insights that can be extended to the rest of healthcare quite easily. I ask point blank: Pfizer is a gigantic, global biopharma company…Why wouldn’t it do these types of innovations internally, in-house themselves? Why partner outside? 

Kathy explains the magic that is unlocked when a “market force” partners with a “market mover” for the sake of innovation, and the lessons learned are far reaching and applicable no matter where you are in health innovation. 

How is Pfizer looking at new models for collaborating with biotech companies? What are the key characteristics of Pfizer’s culture of innovation that have newly emerged or deepened as a result of their work on the Covid vaccine during the pandemic? We dive deep into the biopharma-biotech model and all it brings in terms of new science, breakthrough therapies, and brand-new business opportunities. Watch now!  

*** 
Jessica DaMassa, the emerging ‘It girl’ of health tech interviewing, chats it up with the ‘who’s who’ of the health tech and healthcare innovation set on 'WTF Health - What's the Future, Health?' Catch 100's of interviews with leading health tech startups and the VC investors, health insurance companies, big pharma co's, and hospital systems helping bring their new ideas into the healthcare establishment. From AI and Big Data to digital health, virtual care, telehealth, digital therapeutics, payment model innovation, and investing, Jessica helps you spot the trends and figure out what’s next. 

Subscribe to WTF Health’s YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/wtfhealth 
Follow Jess DaMassa on Twitter: https://twitter.com/jessdamassa
Visit WTF Health: https://www.wtf.health

"WTF Health - What's the Future, Health?" is sponsored in part by OneDrop, Pfizer, Wheel, Vida Health, 120/80 MKTG, Komodo Health, Crossover Health, Newtopia, & Bayer G4A.

snippet.title: 5th European AI Forum, Live from Paris
snippet.description:
We are happy to invite you to our next European AI Forum on June 30th - our flagship conference in collaboration with nine national AI-associations in the EU. This time our friends from Hub France IA are in Charge and the event will be broadcasted from Paris.

0:00 Welcome
3:02 Oleksandr Bornyakov, Deputy Minister of digital transformation, Ukraine 
7:10 Volker Wissing, Federal Minister for Digital and Transport, Germany
13:18 What is the EAIF ?

22:10 Investments in European AI & DeepTech Companies - The perspective of the European Innovation Council - Kaloyan Ratchev (AI Bulgaria) in conversation with Dr. Albena Kuyumdzhieva, Policy Adviser at the EIC Board

35:04 Round table : Finance and compliance - Jeannette Gorzala, Head of Legal and Regulatory at AI Austria with Léa Deleris, Head of RISK Artificial Intelligence Research at BNP Paribas France and Hrvoje Cosic, CEO of Aircash

1:02:33 Renauld Vedel, French National Coordinator for Artificial Intelligence


Chapter 1: Defense

1:17:38 High Level Conference on Responsible Military AI 2023 by Maarten Wammes, Project Lead New Technologies and Arms Control, Security Policy Department NL 

1:28:35 ELSA LAB Defense, Jurriaan van Diggelen, Senior researcher Artificial Intelligence and Program leader Human-machine Teaming

1:38:28 Panel 1: Defense with Gordan Pesic (Board member DOK ING) Victor Vuillard (Chief Security Officer and Chief Technical Officer at Parrot, France), Gundbert Scherf (Co-Founder and Managing Director at Helsing - AI) 

2:16:09 Jiří Šedivý, Chief Executive of the European Defence Agency

Chapter 2: AI and Data Regulation

2:24:17 Kim van Sparrentak (Netherlands) MEP Green Party
2:30:27 Karlo Ressler (Croatia) MEP EPP
2:32:42 Eva Maydell (Bulgaria) MEP EPP 

2:37:04 Panel 2 - AI Data and Regulation with Patrick Bezombes (Chairman of the Artificial Intelligence Standardisation Committee France), Alexandra Ebert (Chief Trust Officer at Mostly AI), Karine Perset (Head of the OECD.AI Observatory)

3:42:17 Florian Tursky, State Secretary Austria
3:44:54 Johann Saathoff, State Secretary, Federal Ministry of Interior, Germany

3:51:13 Panel 3 - Energy: Pedro Gomes Lopes (COO of the The Swarm Initiative), Antoine Couret (President Hub France IA & President at ALEIA), Clemens Wasner (Co-founder & Chairperson AI Austria)

4:42:20 LEAM, Jörg Bienert, President of the German AI Association
4:48:02 Bogdan Ponomar, CEO of the AI House in Kiev Ukraine
4:54:22 Sylwia Stefaniak, Expert, The Chancellery of The Prime Minister in Poland
5:01:32 What is AI for good? Frederic Werner Head, Strategic Engagement "AI for good "
5:12:04 Closing Remarks

snippet.title: agilon's CEO Steve Sell Discusses Total Care Model at National Primary Care Transformation Summit
snippet.description:
“The current model for primary care that’s based on a fee-for-service system is woefully broken. We really built a new primary care model that allows primary care physicians to practice medicine the way they were trained and take the total responsibility for cost, quality, and experience for their patients.”

At the National Primary Care Transformation Summit in July 2022, our CEO, Steven Sell, sat down with John Kirk, founding CEO of Pioneer Medical Group, Inc., to share how our team is building a new primary care model that provides physicians with the insights, time, and tools to focus on the entire health of their senior patients and transform health care in their communities.

snippet.title: DeFi: The Future of Decentralized Governance | WEF’22 Affiliate Session | #HCLTechAtDavos
snippet.description:
How do we bridge the gap between decentralized finance and traditional financial services? De-Fi has experienced exponential growth and it begins its journey into the mainstream, the question is how this alternative model can work with traditional financial markets and services.

snippet.title: 2022 CED Distinguished Leadership Awards: Indra K. Nooyi
snippet.description:
Indra K. Nooyi, the former Chairman and CEO of PepsiCo, introduces award honoree Penny Pennington, Managing Partner of Edward Jones.

About the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards:
https://www.conference-board.org/events/CED-Distinguished-Leadership-Awards-Dinner

For more than a quarter century, the CED Distinguished Leadership Awards have been honoring business leaders and their companies who exemplify CED’s mission by answering the call for corporate citizenship and demonstrating a strong commitment to public policy in the nation’s interests.

The CED Annual Distinguished Leadership Awards Celebration pays special tribute to business leaders and their companies who have demonstrated exceptional leadership in very challenging times and have helped our nation and the communities where they operate navigate the many challenges before us, including: defeating the pandemic, ensuring an economic recovery that provides equal opportunity for all Americans building a more civil and just society and upholding a rules-based international order.

The Awards Celebration commemorates the legacy of CED’s founders, America’s leading CEOs who, during WWII, heeded the call to public service to guide the transition from a wartime economy to a prosperous peacetime economy. Today, CED is fulfilling its mission by working to help ensure the nation recovers from the current crises with an economy that provides equal opportunity for all Americans.
